api/lumify.api.Controllers.AccountContro.title: Class AccountController | Lumify – Code Documentation
api/lumify.api.Controllers.AccountContro.summary: Class AccountController Namespace lumify.api.Controllers Assembly lumify.api.dll Endpoints for user accounts: login/logout, registration, two-factor authentication (TOTP), password reset and e-mail verification. Sessions are issued via an HttpOnly JWT cookie plus a CSRF double-submit cookie. [ApiController] [Route("account/[action]")] public class AccountController : ControllerBase Inheritance object ControllerBase AccountController Constructors AccountController(ILogger<AccountController>, IConfiguration, IWebHostEnvironment, LumifyDbContext, InternalLogic, IEmailService, IOptions<AppSettings>) Creates the controller with its injected dependencies (logging, configuration, hosting environment, database context, internal logic helpers, e-mail service and application settings). public AccountController(ILogger<AccountController> logger, IConfiguration config, IWebHostEnvironment env, LumifyDbContext context, InternalLogic logic, IEmailService emailService, IOptions<AppSettings> appSettings) Parameters logger ILogger<AccountController> config IConfiguration env IWebHostEnvironment context LumifyDbContext logic InternalLogic emailService IEmailService appSettings IOptions<AppSettings> Methods ConfirmTotp(TotpCodeRequest) Confirms 2FA setup: a valid TOTP code flips two-factor authentication on. [HttpPost] [Authorize] [ActionName("confirmTotp")] public Task<IActionResult> ConfirmTotp(TotpCodeRequest dto) Parameters dto TotpCodeRequest The 6-digit code from the authenticator app. Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with enabled = true on success; 400 if the code is missing, setup was not started, or the code is invalid; 404 if the user no longer exists. DisableTotp(TotpCodeRequest) Disables 2FA. Requires a valid current TOTP code so a hijacked session cannot silently turn it off. [HttpPost] [Authorize] [ActionName("disableTotp")] public Task<IActionResult> DisableTotp(TotpCodeRequest dto) Parameters dto TotpCodeRequest The 6-digit code from the authenticator app. Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with enabled = false on success; 400 if the code is missing, 2FA is not enabled, or the code is invalid; 404 if the user no longer exists. LoginUser(LoginRequest) Authenticates a user by username or e-mail and password. [HttpPost] [ActionName("loginUser")] public Task<IActionResult> LoginUser(LoginRequest dto) Parameters dto LoginRequest Credentials: Identifier (username or e-mail) and password. Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with user data (session in cookie) on success without 2FA; 200 with mfaRequired = true and an MFA token when 2FA is enabled; 401 for invalid credentials; 403 when the e-mail address is not yet confirmed. Remarks If 2FA is enabled, no session is issued yet — a short-lived MFA challenge token is returned instead and the client must call VerifyTotpLogin(VerifyTotpLoginRequest) with the 6-digit code. Soft-deleted and unconfirmed accounts cannot log in. LogoutUser() Logs the user out by expiring the session and CSRF cookies immediately. [HttpPost] [ActionName("logoutUser")] public IActionResult LogoutUser() Returns IActionResult 200 once the cookies have been cleared. MfaStatus() Returns whether 2FA is currently active for the logged-in user. [HttpGet] [Authorize] [ActionName("mfaStatus")] public Task<IActionResult> MfaStatus() Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with enabled (bool); 404 if the user no longer exists. RegisterUser(RegisterRequest, CancellationToken) Registers a new user account and sends an e-mail verification link. No automatic login — the account must confirm its e-mail before it can sign in. [HttpPost] [ActionName("registerUser")] public Task<IActionResult> RegisterUser(RegisterRequest dto, CancellationToken ct) Parameters dto RegisterRequest Registration data (e-mail, username, password, optional name/avatar). ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with verificationRequired = true on success; 400 if required fields are missing; 409 if the username or e-mail already exists; 500 on error. Remarks An optional base64 avatar is stored; failure to save it is logged but does not abort registration (a default avatar is used instead). RequestPasswordReset(RequestPasswordResetRequest, CancellationToken) Password reset step 1: requests a reset link for the given username or e-mail. [HttpPost] [ActionName("requestPasswordReset")] public Task<IActionResult> RequestPasswordReset(RequestPasswordResetRequest dto, CancellationToken ct) Parameters dto RequestPasswordResetRequest The identifier (username or e-mail). ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with a generic message in all cases. Remarks Always returns the same generic message regardless of whether the account exists, so the endpoint cannot be used to enumerate registered users. Any previously open reset tokens for the user are invalidated; only the hash of the new token is stored. ResendVerification(ResendVerificationRequest, CancellationToken) Resends a verification mail for an existing, still-unconfirmed account. [HttpPost] [ActionName("resendVerification")] public Task<IActionResult> ResendVerification(ResendVerificationRequest dto, CancellationToken ct) Parameters dto ResendVerificationRequest The identifier (username or e-mail). ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with a generic message in all cases. Remarks Always answers with the same generic message (no user enumeration); a mail is only actually sent for an account that exists and is not yet confirmed. ResetPassword(ResetPasswordRequest, CancellationToken) Password reset step 2: sets a new password using the single-use token from the reset link. [HttpPost] [ActionName("resetPassword")] public Task<IActionResult> ResetPassword(ResetPasswordRequest dto, CancellationToken ct) Parameters dto ResetPasswordRequest The reset token and the new password (minimum 8 characters). ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with a success message; 400 if input is missing, the password is too short, or the token is invalid, already used, or expired. Remarks The token is looked up by its hash (the raw value is never stored) and consumed on success, so each reset link works only once and only before it expires. SetupTotp() Starts 2FA setup: generates a fresh TOTP secret and a QR code. 2FA is not active until confirmed via ConfirmTotp(TotpCodeRequest). [HttpPost] [Authorize] [ActionName("setupTotp")] public Task<IActionResult> SetupTotp() Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with secret (for manual entry) and qrCodeDataUri (for scanning); 400 if 2FA is already enabled; 404 if the user no longer exists. VerifyEmail(VerifyEmailRequest, CancellationToken) Confirms an account via the single-use token from the verification mail. [HttpPost] [ActionName("verifyEmail")] public Task<IActionResult> VerifyEmail(VerifyEmailRequest dto, CancellationToken ct) Parameters dto VerifyEmailRequest The verification token. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with a success message; 400 if the token is missing, invalid, already used, or expired. Remarks The token is looked up by its hash and consumed on success, so the verification link works only once and only before it expires. VerifyTotpLogin(VerifyTotpLoginRequest) Login step 2: verifies the 6-digit TOTP code for a 2FA-enabled account and then issues the real session. [HttpPost] [ActionName("verifyTotpLogin")] public Task<IActionResult> VerifyTotpLogin(VerifyTotpLoginRequest dto) Parameters dto VerifyTotpLoginRequest The MFA challenge token from step 1 and the 6-digit code. Returns Task<IActionResult> 200 with user data and the session cookie on success; 400 if token or code are missing; 401 if the MFA session or the code is invalid or expired. WhoAmI() Diagnostic endpoint that returns the current authentication state and the claims of the logged-in user. [HttpGet] [Authorize] [ActionName("whoami")] public IActionResult WhoAmI() Returns IActionResult 200 with Authenticated (bool) and the list of Claims.

api/lumify.api.Controllers.ChatControlle.title: Class ChatController | Lumify – Code Documentation
api/lumify.api.Controllers.ChatControlle.summary: Class ChatController Namespace lumify.api.Controllers Assembly lumify.api.dll Read access to chat history. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Real-time delivery of new messages is handled by ChatHub; this controller only serves the persisted message history. [ApiController] [Route("chat/[action]")] [Authorize] public class ChatController : ControllerBase Inheritance object ControllerBase ChatController Constructors ChatController(ILogger<ChatController>, LumifyDbContext) Creates the controller with its injected logger and database context. public ChatController(ILogger<ChatController> logger, LumifyDbContext db) Parameters logger ILogger<ChatController> db LumifyDbContext Methods GetMessagesOfRoom(string, CancellationToken) Returns the full message history of a chat room, oldest first. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getMessagesOfRoom")] public Task<ActionResult<List<ChatMessageResponse>>> GetMessagesOfRoom(string roomID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters roomID string The room whose messages are requested. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<ChatMessageResponse>>> 200 with the list of messages; 400 if roomID is missing. Remarks Soft-deleted messages and messages from soft-deleted users are excluded. The sender name is resolved to the user's display name (falling back to username, full name, or ID).

api/lumify.api.Controllers.EventsControl.title: Class EventsController | Lumify – Code Documentation
api/lumify.api.Controllers.EventsControl.summary: Class EventsController Namespace lumify.api.Controllers Assembly lumify.api.dll Manages calendar events, both personal (no workspace) and workspace-shared, including creation, editing, deletion and various queries (counts, lists, linked workspace). All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes to workspace events are pushed live to the workspace group over the EventHub. [ApiController] [Route("events/[action]")] [Authorize] public class EventsController : ControllerBase Inheritance object ControllerBase EventsController Remarks Permission rule throughout: a personal event may only be modified by its owner, while a workspace event may be modified by any active member of that workspace (see CanModify). For privacy, the creator is surfaced only as display name → username → ID, never the real first/last name. Constructors EventsController(ILogger<EventsController>, LumifyDbContext, IHubContext<EventHub>) Creates the controller with its injected logger, database context and the event hub used for live notifications. public EventsController(ILogger<EventsController> logger, LumifyDbContext db, IHubContext<EventHub> eventHub) Parameters logger ILogger<EventsController> db LumifyDbContext eventHub IHubContext<EventHub> Methods AddEvent(AddEventRequest, CancellationToken) Creates a new calendar event, optionally inside a workspace. [HttpPost] [ActionName("addEvent")] public Task<ActionResult<EventResponse>> AddEvent(AddEventRequest request, CancellationToken ct) Parameters request AddEventRequest Event data (name, start/end time required; optional description, all-day, workspace). ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<EventResponse>> 200 with the created event; 400 on missing/invalid fields; 403 if the user is not a member of the target workspace. Remarks Start and end times must be valid dates and end must not be before start. If a workspace is given, the user must be a member of it. On success in a workspace, an EventCreated event is broadcast to the workspace group. DeleteEvent(string, CancellationToken) Soft-deletes a calendar event. [HttpDelete] [ActionName("deleteEvent")] public Task<ActionResult> DeleteEvent(string eventID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters eventID string The event to delete. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult> 200 with success = true and the event ID; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user may not delete it; 404 if the event does not exist. Remarks The record is kept and marked with DeletedAt. On a workspace event, an EventDeleted event is broadcast to the workspace group. GetAllEventsOfUser(CancellationToken) Returns all personal events (no workspace) of the current user, ordered by start date. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getAllEventsOfUser")] public Task<ActionResult<List<EventResponse>>> GetAllEventsOfUser(CancellationToken ct) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<EventResponse>>> 200 with the list of events. GetAllEventsOfWorkspace(string, CancellationToken) Returns all events of a workspace, ordered by start date. The user must be a member. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getAllEventsOfWorkspace")] public Task<ActionResult<List<EventResponse>>> GetAllEventsOfWorkspace(string workspaceID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters workspaceID string The workspace whose events are requested. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<EventResponse>>> 200 with the list of events; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user is not a member; 404 if the workspace does not exist. Remarks Events whose creator was soft-deleted are kept (content belongs to the workspace) and surface the creator as "Gelöschter Benutzer". GetEventCountOfUser(CancellationToken) Returns the number of personal events (no workspace) owned by the current user. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getEventCountOfUser")] public Task<ActionResult<int>> GetEventCountOfUser(CancellationToken ct) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<int>> 200 with the event count. GetEventCountOfWorkspace(string, CancellationToken) Returns the number of events in a workspace. The user must be a member. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getEventCountOfWorkspace")] public Task<ActionResult<int>> GetEventCountOfWorkspace(string workspaceID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters workspaceID string The workspace to count events for. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<int>> 200 with the event count; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user is not a member; 404 if the workspace does not exist. GetSpaceInfosOfEvent(string, CancellationToken) Returns the workspace that the given event belongs to, if any. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getSpaceInfosOfEvent")] public Task<ActionResult<WorkspaceResponse>> GetSpaceInfosOfEvent(string eventID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters eventID string The event whose workspace is requested. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<WorkspaceResponse>> 200 with the workspace; 400 if the ID is missing; 404 if the event has no linked workspace or it was deleted. SaveEvent(SaveEventRequest, CancellationToken) Updates an event's name, description, all-day flag and/or start/end times. Only the provided fields are changed, and the database is only written if something changed. [HttpPatch] [ActionName("saveEvent")] public Task<ActionResult<EventResponse>> SaveEvent(SaveEventRequest request, CancellationToken ct) Parameters request SaveEventRequest The event ID plus the fields to change. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<EventResponse>> 200 with the updated event; 400 on invalid input; 403 if the user may not modify it; 404 if the event does not exist. Remarks On a successful change in a workspace, an EventUpdated event is broadcast to the workspace group.

api/lumify.api.Controllers.FoldersContro.title: Class FoldersController | Lumify – Code Documentation
api/lumify.api.Controllers.FoldersContro.summary: Class FoldersController Namespace lumify.api.Controllers Assembly lumify.api.dll Manages note folders, both personal (no workspace) and workspace-shared, including creation, editing, moving and recursive deletion. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes to workspace folders are pushed live to the workspace group over the NoteHub. [ApiController] [Route("folders/[action]")] [Authorize] public class FoldersController : ControllerBase Inheritance object ControllerBase FoldersController Remarks Permission rule throughout: a personal folder may only be modified by its owner, while a workspace folder may be modified by any active member of that workspace (see CanModify). Constructors FoldersController(ILogger<FoldersController>, LumifyDbContext, IHubContext<NoteHub>) Creates the controller with its injected logger, database context and the note hub used for live notifications. public FoldersController(ILogger<FoldersController> logger, LumifyDbContext db, IHubContext<NoteHub> noteHub) Parameters logger ILogger<FoldersController> db LumifyDbContext noteHub IHubContext<NoteHub> Methods AddFolder(AddFolderRequest, CancellationToken) Creates a new folder, optionally inside a workspace and/or a parent folder. [HttpPost] [ActionName("addFolder")] public Task<ActionResult<FolderResponse>> AddFolder(AddFolderRequest request, CancellationToken ct) Parameters request AddFolderRequest Folder data (name required; optional description, workspace, parent). ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<FolderResponse>> 200 with the created folder; 400 on missing name or invalid workspace/parent; 403 if the user is not a member of the target workspace or owner of the parent. Remarks If a workspace is given, the user must be a member of it; if a parent folder is given, it must live in the same space. On success in a workspace, a FolderCreated event is broadcast to the workspace group. DeleteFolder(string, CancellationToken) Soft-deletes a folder together with all of its descendant sub-folders and their notes (recursive, breadth-first). [HttpDelete] [ActionName("deleteFolder")] public Task<ActionResult> DeleteFolder(string folderID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters folderID string The root folder to delete. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult> 200 with success = true and the folder ID; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user may not delete it; 404 if the folder does not exist. Remarks Nothing is physically removed — all affected rows get a DeletedAt timestamp. On a workspace folder, a FolderDeleted event is broadcast to the workspace group. GetAllFoldersOfUser(CancellationToken) Returns all personal folders of the current user (no workspace), oldest first. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getAllFoldersOfUser")] public Task<ActionResult<List<FolderResponse>>> GetAllFoldersOfUser(CancellationToken ct) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<FolderResponse>>> 200 with the list of personal folders. GetAllFoldersOfWorkspace(string, CancellationToken) Returns all folders of the given workspace, oldest first. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getAllFoldersOfWorkspace")] public Task<ActionResult<List<FolderResponse>>> GetAllFoldersOfWorkspace(string workspaceID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters workspaceID string The workspace whose folders are requested. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<FolderResponse>>> 200 with the list of workspace folders. SaveFolder(SaveFolderRequest, CancellationToken) Updates a folder's name, description and/or parent (move). Only the provided fields are changed; the database is only written if something actually changed. [HttpPatch] [ActionName("saveFolder")] public Task<ActionResult<FolderResponse>> SaveFolder(SaveFolderRequest request, CancellationToken ct) Parameters request SaveFolderRequest The folder ID plus the fields to change. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<FolderResponse>> 200 with the updated folder; 400 on invalid input or move target; 403 if the user may not modify it; 404 if the folder does not exist. Remarks Move rules: a folder cannot be moved into itself; a personal folder may only move into another personal folder of the same owner; a workspace folder may only move within the same workspace. On a successful change in a workspace, a FolderUpdated event is broadcast to the workspace group.

api/lumify.api.Controllers.FriendshipCon.title: Class FriendshipController | Lumify – Code Documentation
api/lumify.api.Controllers.FriendshipCon.summary: Class FriendshipController Namespace lumify.api.Controllers Assembly lumify.api.dll Manages friendships: sending, accepting and rejecting friend requests, removing friends, and listing incoming/outgoing requests and current friends. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes are pushed live to the affected user over the ChatHub so clients update without a reload. [ApiController] [Route("friendship/[action]")] [Authorize] public class FriendshipController : ControllerBase Inheritance object ControllerBase FriendshipController Constructors FriendshipController(ILogger<FriendshipController>, LumifyDbContext, FriendshipService, IPresenceService, IHubContext<ChatHub>) Creates the controller with its injected dependencies (logging, database context, friendship service, presence service and the chat hub used for live notifications). public FriendshipController(ILogger<FriendshipController> logger, LumifyDbContext db, FriendshipService friendshipService, IPresenceService presenceService, IHubContext<ChatHub> chatHub) Parameters logger ILogger<FriendshipController> db LumifyDbContext friendshipService FriendshipService presenceService IPresenceService chatHub IHubContext<ChatHub> Methods AcceptFriendRequest(string) Accepts a pending friend request addressed to the current user. [HttpPatch] [ActionName("acceptFriendRequest")] public Task<ActionResult> AcceptFriendRequest(string friendshipID) Parameters friendshipID string The pending friendship to accept. Returns Task<ActionResult> 200 with Success = true; 400 if the ID is missing or the request cannot be accepted (e.g. not addressed to the current user). GetFriendsOfUser(CancellationToken) Returns the current user's accepted friends, each enriched with its live presence status (online/away/offline) from the presence service. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getFriendsOfUser")] public Task<ActionResult<List<UserPreviewResponse>>> GetFriendsOfUser(CancellationToken ct) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<UserPreviewResponse>>> 200 with the list of friends; 401 if no user is logged in. GetIncomingFriendRequests(CancellationToken) Returns the pending friend requests addressed to the current user, newest first, including the requester's username, display name and avatar. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getIncomingFriendRequests")] public Task<ActionResult<List<FriendshipResponse>>> GetIncomingFriendRequests(CancellationToken ct) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<FriendshipResponse>>> 200 with the list of incoming requests; 401 if no user is logged in. GetOutgoingFriendRequests(CancellationToken) Returns the pending friend requests sent by the current user, newest first, including the addressee's username, display name and avatar. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getOutgoingFriendRequests")] public Task<ActionResult<List<FriendshipResponse>>> GetOutgoingFriendRequests(CancellationToken ct) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<FriendshipResponse>>> 200 with the list of outgoing requests; 401 if no user is logged in. RejectFriendRequest(string) Rejects a pending friend request addressed to the current user. [HttpPatch] [ActionName("rejectFriendRequest")] public Task<ActionResult> RejectFriendRequest(string friendshipID) Parameters friendshipID string The pending friendship to reject. Returns Task<ActionResult> 200 with Success = true; 400 if the ID is missing or the request cannot be rejected. RemoveFriend(string) Removes an existing friendship between the current user and another user. [HttpPatch] [ActionName("removeFriend")] public Task<ActionResult> RemoveFriend(string friendID) Parameters friendID string The friend to remove. Returns Task<ActionResult> 200 with Success = true; 400 if the ID is missing or the friendship cannot be removed. SendFriendRequest(string) Sends a friend request from the current user to another user. [HttpPost] [ActionName("sendFriendRequest")] public Task<ActionResult> SendFriendRequest(string addresseeID) Parameters addresseeID string The user who should receive the request. Returns Task<ActionResult> 200 with Success = true; 400 if the ID is missing or the request is rejected by the friendship service (e.g. already friends or pending).

api/lumify.api.Controllers.NotesControll.title: Class NotesController | Lumify – Code Documentation
api/lumify.api.Controllers.NotesControll.summary: Class NotesController Namespace lumify.api.Controllers Assembly lumify.api.dll Manages notes and their content modules — text blocks and link items — both personal (no workspace) and workspace-shared. Covers creating, editing, moving, ordering and deleting notes and modules, plus various queries. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes to workspace notes are pushed live to the workspace group over the NoteHub. [ApiController] [Route("notes/[action]")] [Authorize] public class NotesController : ControllerBase Inheritance object ControllerBase NotesController Remarks A note is a container; its body is an ordered list of modules positioned via NotePos. Deleting a note cascades a soft-delete to all of its modules and attachments. Permission rule throughout: a personal note may only be modified by its owner, a workspace note by any active member (see CanModify). Constructors NotesController(ILogger<NotesController>, LumifyDbContext, IHubContext<NoteHub>) Creates the controller with its injected logger, database context and the note hub used for live notifications. public NotesController(ILogger<NotesController> logger, LumifyDbContext db, IHubContext<NoteHub> noteHub) Parameters logger ILogger<NotesController> db LumifyDbContext noteHub IHubContext<NoteHub> Methods AddLinkItem(AddLinkItemRequest, CancellationToken) Appends a link item module (labelled URL) to a note, placed at the end (next free NotePos). [HttpPost] [ActionName("addLinkItem")] public Task<ActionResult<LinkItemResponse>> AddLinkItem(AddLinkItemRequest request, CancellationToken ct) Parameters request AddLinkItemRequest Link item data including the parent note ID, URL and optional label. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<LinkItemResponse>> 200 with the created link item; 400 if the note ID or URL is missing; 403 if the user may not modify the note; 404 if the note does not exist. Remarks On success in a workspace, a LinkItemCreated event is broadcast to the workspace group. AddNote(AddNoteRequest, CancellationToken) Creates a new (empty) note, optionally inside a workspace and/or a folder. [HttpPost] [ActionName("addNote")] public Task<ActionResult<NoteResponse>> AddNote(AddNoteRequest request, CancellationToken ct) Parameters request AddNoteRequest Note data (name required; optional workspace and folder). ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<NoteResponse>> 200 with the created note; 400 on missing name or invalid workspace/folder; 403 if the user is not a member of the target workspace or owner of the folder. Remarks If a workspace is given, the user must be a member; if a folder is given, it must live in the same space. On success in a workspace, a NoteCreated event is broadcast to the workspace group. AddTextBlock(AddTextBlockRequest, CancellationToken) Appends a text block module (text, heading, code, …) to a note, placed at the end (next free NotePos). [HttpPost] [ActionName("addTextBlock")] public Task<ActionResult<TextblockResponse>> AddTextBlock(AddTextBlockRequest request, CancellationToken ct) Parameters request AddTextBlockRequest Text block data including the parent note ID, type and content. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<TextblockResponse>> 200 with the created text block; 400 if the note ID is missing; 403 if the user may not modify the note; 404 if the note does not exist. Remarks On success in a workspace, a TextBlockCreated event is broadcast to the workspace group. DeleteLinkItem(string, CancellationToken) Soft-deletes a single link item from its note. [HttpDelete] [ActionName("deleteLinkItem")] public Task<ActionResult> DeleteLinkItem(string linkItemID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters linkItemID string The link item to delete. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult> 200 with success = true and the link item ID; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user may not modify the note; 404 if the link item or its parent note does not exist. Remarks On a workspace note, a LinkItemDeleted event is broadcast to the workspace group; the parent note's timestamp is bumped. DeleteNote(string, CancellationToken) Soft-deletes a note together with all of its modules (text blocks, link items) and attachments. [HttpDelete] [ActionName("deleteNote")] public Task<ActionResult> DeleteNote(string noteID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters noteID string The note to delete. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult> 200 with success = true and the note ID; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user may not delete it; 404 if the note does not exist. Remarks Everything is kept and marked with DeletedAt. On a workspace note, a NoteDeleted event is broadcast to the workspace group. DeleteTextBlock(string, CancellationToken) Soft-deletes a single text block from its note. [HttpDelete] [ActionName("deleteTextBlock")] public Task<ActionResult> DeleteTextBlock(string textblockID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters textblockID string The text block to delete. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult> 200 with success = true and the text block ID; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user may not modify the note; 404 if the block or its parent note does not exist. Remarks On a workspace note, a TextBlockDeleted event is broadcast to the workspace group; the parent note's timestamp is bumped. GetAllNotesOfUser(CancellationToken) Returns all personal notes (no workspace) of the current user, oldest first. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getAllNotesOfUser")] public Task<ActionResult<List<NoteResponse>>> GetAllNotesOfUser(CancellationToken ct) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<NoteResponse>>> 200 with the list of notes. GetAllNotesOfWorkspace(string, CancellationToken) Returns all notes of a workspace, oldest first. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getAllNotesOfWorkspace")] public Task<ActionResult<List<NoteResponse>>> GetAllNotesOfWorkspace(string workspaceID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters workspaceID string The workspace whose notes are requested. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<NoteResponse>>> 200 with the list of notes. Remarks Notes whose creator was soft-deleted are kept (content belongs to the workspace) and surface the creator as "Gelöschter Benutzer". GetLinkItemsOfNote(string, CancellationToken) Returns all link items of a note, in display order (NotePos). [HttpGet] [ActionName("getLinkItemsOfNote")] public Task<ActionResult<List<LinkItemResponse>>> GetLinkItemsOfNote(string noteID, CancellationToken ct) Parameters noteID string The note whose link items are requested. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<List<LinkItemResponse>>> 200 with the ordered link items; 400 if the ID is missing; 404 if the note does not exist. GetNoteCountOfUser(CancellationToken) Returns the number of personal notes (no workspace) owned by the current user. [HttpGet] [ActionName("getNoteCountOfUser")] public Task<ActionResult<int>> GetNoteCountOfUser(CancellationToken ct) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancellation token for the request. Returns Task<ActionResult<int>> 200 with the note count. GetNoteCountOfWorkspace(string, CancellationToken) Returns the number of notes in a workspace. The user must be a member. api/lumify.api.Controllers.html.title: Namespace lumify.api.Controllers | Lumify – Code Documentation
api/lumify.api.Controllers.html.summary: Namespace lumify.api.Controllers Classes AccountController Endpoints for user accounts: login/logout, registration, two-factor authentication (TOTP), password reset and e-mail verification. Sessions are issued via an HttpOnly JWT cookie plus a CSRF double-submit cookie. ChatController Read access to chat history. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Real-time delivery of new messages is handled by ChatHub; this controller only serves the persisted message history. EventsController Manages calendar events, both personal (no workspace) and workspace-shared, including creation, editing, deletion and various queries (counts, lists, linked workspace). All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes to workspace events are pushed live to the workspace group over the EventHub. FoldersController Manages note folders, both personal (no workspace) and workspace-shared, including creation, editing, moving and recursive deletion. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes to workspace folders are pushed live to the workspace group over the NoteHub. FriendshipController Manages friendships: sending, accepting and rejecting friend requests, removing friends, and listing incoming/outgoing requests and current friends. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes are pushed live to the affected user over the ChatHub so clients update without a reload. NotesController Manages notes and their content modules — text blocks and link items — both personal (no workspace) and workspace-shared. Covers creating, editing, moving, ordering and deleting notes and modules, plus various queries. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes to workspace notes are pushed live to the workspace group over the NoteHub. TodoListsController Manages todo lists and their entries, both personal (no workspace) and workspace-shared, including creation, editing, deletion and various queries. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes to workspace items are pushed live to the workspace group over the TodoHub. UsersController User profile and account management plus user discovery: editing the current user's profile and avatar, deleting the account, reading profile/account/preview data by ID or for the current user, recent activity (todos/events/notes), related users, and user search. All endpoints require an authenticated user. WorkspaceController Manages workspaces (shared spaces) and their memberships: creating, renaming and deleting workspaces, adding and removing members, and querying workspaces and members. All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes are pushed live to every affected member over the WorkspaceHub.

api/lumify.api.Hubs.ChatHub.html.summary: Class ChatHub Namespace lumify.api.Hubs Assembly lumify.api.dll SignalR hub for real-time chat and user presence. Tracks each user's active connections via the presence service, broadcasts presence changes to all clients, and handles joining rooms, leaving rooms and sending messages. Requires an authenticated connection. [Authorize] public class ChatHub : Hub Inheritance object Hub ChatHub Remarks Presence is connection-counted: a user is online while at least one connection is open. api/lumify.api.Models.EF.html.title: Namespace lumify.api.Models.EF | Lumify – Code Documentation
api/lumify.api.Models.EF.html.summary: Namespace lumify.api.Models.EF Classes ChatMessage A single chat message in a room. Persisted by ChatHub and read back via ChatController. Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. EmailVerificationToken A single-use token backing an e-mail verification link. Only the hash of the token is stored (same approach as PasswordResetToken), so a leaked row cannot be turned back into a working link. Event A calendar event, either personal (WorkspaceID null) or workspace-shared. Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. Folder A folder that organizes notes, either personal or workspace-shared. Folders can be nested via ParentFolderID. Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. Friendship A relationship between two users (friend request or accepted friendship). Stored once per user pair using ordered UserLowID/UserHighID keys; the requester/addressee fields record who initiated it. See FriendshipService. Note A note container, either personal or workspace-shared, optionally filed in a folder. Its body is an ordered list of modules (text blocks and link items). Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. NoteAttachment A file attached to a note. Stores the upload metadata and the name under which the file is stored on disk. Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. Note_LinkItem A link item module within a note — a labelled URL. Ordered within the note via NotePos. Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. Note_TextBlock A text block module within a note (e.g. paragraph, heading or code block). Ordered within the note via NotePos. Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. PasswordResetToken A single-use token backing a password-reset link. Only the hash of the token is stored, so a leaked row cannot be turned back into a working reset link. TodoEntry A single item within a TodoList. Toggling its status keeps the parent list's status in sync. Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. TodoList A todo list, either personal or workspace-shared, holding a set of TodoEntry items. Its status is derived from its entries (done when all entries are done). Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. User A registered user account. The central entity most other content references via its owner. Soft-deleted via DeletedAt; timestamps are stored as ISO-8601 strings. Workspace A shared space that groups content (folders, notes, todo lists, events) and members. Content belongs to the workspace, not its creator. Soft-deleted via DeletedAt. WorkspaceMember Join entity linking a User to a Workspace with a role. A removed membership is soft-deleted via DeletedAt. Enums Role The role of a User, persisted to the database by name.

api/lumify.api.Services.FriendshipServic.title: Class FriendshipService | Lumify – Code Documentation
api/lumify.api.Services.FriendshipServic.summary: Class FriendshipService Namespace lumify.api.Services Assembly lumify.api.dll Encapsulates the friendship domain logic used by the FriendshipController: sending, accepting, rejecting and removing friendships, including the validation and state transitions involved. public class FriendshipService Inheritance object FriendshipService Remarks Each friendship is stored once per user pair using ordered UserLowID/UserHighID keys (a plain unique index, since MariaDB has no filtered indexes), so a soft-deleted row is resurrected rather than duplicated. Invalid operations throw, and the controller maps those to error responses. Constructors FriendshipService(LumifyDbContext) Creates the service with its injected database context. public FriendshipService(LumifyDbContext db) Parameters db LumifyDbContext Methods AcceptFriendRequest(string, string) Accepts a pending friend request. Only the addressee may accept, and only pending requests can be accepted. public Task<string> AcceptFriendRequest(string friendshipID, string currentUserID) Parameters friendshipID string The pending friendship to accept. currentUserID string The user performing the action (must be the addressee). Returns Task<string> The requester's ID, so the caller can notify the counterpart of the change. Exceptions Exception Thrown if an ID is missing, the friendship is not found, the caller is not the addressee, or the request is not pending. RejectFriendRequest(string, string) Rejects a pending friend request. Only the addressee may reject, and only pending requests can be rejected. public Task<string> RejectFriendRequest(string friendshipID, string currentUserID) Parameters friendshipID string The pending friendship to reject. currentUserID string The user performing the action (must be the addressee). Returns Task<string> The requester's ID, so the caller can notify the counterpart of the change. Exceptions Exception Thrown if an ID is missing, the friendship is not found, the caller is not the addressee, or the request is not pending. RemoveFriend(string, string) Removes an accepted friendship by soft-deleting it. Only accepted friendships can be removed. public Task RemoveFriend(string currentUserID, string friendID) Parameters currentUserID string The user performing the removal. friendID string The friend to remove. Returns Task Exceptions Exception Thrown if an ID is missing, the user removes themselves, the friendship is not found, or it is not in the accepted state. SendFriendRequest(string, string) Creates a pending friend request from one user to another, or resurrects a previously removed relationship as a fresh request. public Task SendFriendRequest(string requesterID, string addresseeID) Parameters requesterID string The user sending the request. addresseeID string The user receiving the request. Returns Task Exceptions Exception Thrown if an ID is missing, the user adds themselves, or an active friendship already exists.

api/lumify.api.Services.PresenceService..summary: Class PresenceService Namespace lumify.api.Services Assembly lumify.api.dll In-memory implementation of IPresenceService. Maintains the mapping between users and their open SignalR connections in thread-safe dictionaries; a user counts as online while at least one connection is registered. public class PresenceService : IPresenceService Inheritance object PresenceService Implements IPresenceService Remarks State is per-process and not persisted, so it resets on restart and is not shared across multiple server instances. Methods AddConnection(string, string) Registers a new connection for a user, creating the user's connection set on first use. public void AddConnection(string userID, string connectionID) Parameters userID string The connecting user. connectionID string The new SignalR connection ID. GetPresenceStatus(string) Returns whether the user currently has any open connection. public PresenceStatus GetPresenceStatus(string userID) Parameters userID string The user to check. Returns PresenceStatus Online if at least one connection is open, otherwise Offline. RemoveConnection(string) Removes a connection and, if it was the user's last one, drops the user from the online set. public void RemoveConnection(string connectionID) Parameters connectionID string The SignalR connection ID that closed.
